name: BHUMI
project_details:
Comparative Research & Program Improvement for Shelter and SkillEd  Initiatives
Description: The interns will research various organizations that implement programs inshelter homes and SkillEd initiatives. They will visit, observe and analyze how theseprograms are designed and executed. Based on this, interns will conduct a comparativeanalysis to identify best practices, gaps, and innovative approaches. The final outcome willinclude actionable strategies and recommendations that Bhumi can adopt to enhance itsown program implementation.Skill sets: Research and analytical skills, Observation and documentation, Critical thinking,Data interpretation, Report writingExpected learning: The internship will provide a comprehensive understanding of programdesign and implementation in the social sector, along with exposure to diverse models ofeducation and shelter-based interventions. Interns will develop the ability to criticallyevaluate and compare impact-driven programs, enabling them to identify best practicesand gaps. Through this process, they will also gain experience in formulating well-informed,evidence-based recommendations for program improvement.

name: BSNL Zira
project_details:
Title: Design and Performance Analysis of FTTH (Fiber-to-the-Home) Network Infrastructure,Title: Data-driven Optimization of Broadband Service Quality in FTTH Networks
Description: This project focuses on understanding and analysing the deployment of FTTH networksin BSNL. It includes studying the architecture of fiber optic communication systems, components suchas Optical Line Terminal (OLT), Optical Network Terminal (ONT), splitters, and fiber distributionnetworks. The project will involve evaluating network performance in terms of bandwidth, latency, signalattenuation, and reliability in real-world conditions.Skill sets:Basic knowledge of communication systemsUnderstanding of fiber optics fundamentals Data analysis and interpretationExpected learning: End-to-end understanding of FTTH network architecture Practical insights into fiber optic communication systems Analysis of network performance metrics (latency, attenuation, throughput) Exposure to real-world telecom infrastructure and deployment challengesSpecific courses required for project execution: Basic understanding of mathematics and logical reasoning Familiarity with fundamental science concepts (physics/technology),Description: This project aims to analyse broadband usage and network performance data in BSNL’sFTTH services to identify inefficiencies and optimise service delivery. name: Bikaner Ceramics Pvt. Ltd
project_details:
Process Optimization and Thermal Efficiency Analysis in Ceramic Kiln Operations
The project focuses on studying the firing and sintering processes used in the production of porcelain electrical insulators. Students will analyze kiln operating conditions, temperature profiles, and firing cycles to understand their effect on product quality and energy consumption. The project will involve process mapping, identification of inefficiencies, and recommendations for improving thermal efficiency and process stability.Skill sets:  Basic chemical engineering fundamentals, Heat transfer conceptsExpected learning:  Students will gain exposure to industrial thermal processing systems, ceramic sintering mechanisms, kiln operation principles, and energy efficiency considerations in manufacturing processes.Specific courses required for project execution:  Chemical Engineering Thermodynamics, Heat Transfer, Material Sciences and Engineering

name: CSIR - National Aerospace Laboratories
project_details:
Title: Development of Zn-Ni coating on aircraft Al alloys for corrosion protection
Description: Cadmium coating is widely used in aerospace and automotive applicationsfor corrosion protection and lubricity. However, due to its toxic nature there is a need toreplace this coating. Zn-Ni coating is reported to be a most viable alternate to cadmium.The study is aimed to optimize the deposition conditions on aircraft Al alloy and deposit Zn-Ni coating with performance comparable to cadmium plating. A pre-treatment procedureneeds to be optimized to obtain an adherent coating on Al alloy. The developed coatingshall be characterized and evaluated in terms of composition, thickness, adhesion,corrosion resistance and lubricity.Skill sets: Ability to carry out coating, metallographyExpected learning: Development of eco-friendly corrosion protection coating on aircraft Alalloys as alternate to cadmiumSpecific courses required for project execution: Coatings/ Surface modification/Materials Science/

name: CSIR - National Aerospace Laboratories
project_details:
Title: Development of metal support solid oxide fuel cell
Description: Solid oxide electrolysis cells (SOECs) offer the highest electrical efficiencyamong electrolysis technologies, yet conventional ceramic-supported architectures remainbrittle, costly, and vulnerable under thermal cycling. To overcome these barriers, thisproject proposes the development of metal-supported SOECs (MS-SOECs), a next-generation platform that combines high electrochemical performance with mechanicalrobustness, manufacturability, and long-term durability. The present work focuses ondesign and fabrication MS-SOEC. It also involves modeling and simulation materials andflow fields for the MS-SOEC.Skill sets: Software kills on COMSOL, Solidworks, VASP Ab initioExpected learning: Fuel cell design and fabrication, Materials design and modelingSpecific courses required for project execution: Mechanical Engineering Chemical Engineering Materials Engineering Nanotechnology with Physics/ Chemistry background

project_details:
Title: Thermal Barrier coating for small gas turbine engine
Description: Thermal Barrier Coatings (TBCs) are a critical technology in modern gasturbine engines (both for aircraft propulsion and industrial power generation). Their mainpurpose is to protect hot-section components (like turbine blades, vanes, and combustorliners) from extreme operating temperatures, which can exceed the melting point of theunderlying metal alloys. The present work involve development of TBC by plasma sprayingprocess and testing in relevant conditions.Skill sets: Software kills on COMSOL, Solidworks, VASP Ab initioExpected learning: TBC coating techniques, Materials design and modelingSpecific courses required for project execution: Mechanical Engineering Chemical Engineering Materials Engineering Aerospace Engineering Nanotechnology with Physics/ Chemistry background

name: CSIR - National Aerospace Laboratories
project_details:
Title: Development of Generative AI models for Electromagnetic Applications,Title: Generative Adversarial Network based AI models for Data Augmentation in Space  Applications
Description: Traditional design approaches for electromagnetic (EM) components,including antennas and absorbers, depend heavily on parametric optimization, whichinvolves computationally expensive and time-consuming EM simulations. To address thischallenge, deep learning models present an attractive alternative by facilitating automationof the design process. In this work, advanced generative AI models will be developed toenable such capabilities.Skill sets: Programming in Python, Familiarity with AI/ML, Inquisitiveness, Perseverance,Sense of integrityExpected Outcomes: Deep learning models, Technical reports, PublicationsSpecific courses required for project execution: Computer programming/EM FieldTheory, AI/ML,Description: Deep learning algorithms have shown significant promise in automating theinspection of electronic assemblies. However, their effectiveness is often hindered by thescarcity of labeled defect data. While traditional image augmentation offers limitedimprovements, Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) have emerged as a powerfulalternative for synthetic data generation. This project aims to explore and adapt variousGAN architectures, such as Conditional GANs, CycleGANs, and Wasserstein GANs, tosynthetically generate high-quality defect images and augment existing datasets forenhanced deep learning-based inspection.Skill sets: Programming in Python, Familiarity with AI/ML, Inquisitiveness, Perseverance,Sense of integrityExpected Outcomes: Deep learning models, Technical reports, PublicationsSpecific courses required for project execution: Computer programming, AI/ML

project_details:
Title: Development of Smart Fluid Damping System Containing Nano Electro-Rheological  (NER) Materials
Description: The project investigates the application of electrorheological materials to dampingsystems to engineer scientific developments involving Light Weight Smart Micro Systems(LWSMSs), especially in aerospace damping applications. Subsequently, the study involvessynthesis, development and characterisation of ER fluids and design principles to enable thisproperty in order to design a micro damper for Line Replaceable Units (LRUs) by varying theelectrical voltage.Skill sets: Analytical and Design, Modelling – CADExpected learning: The areas of ER fluids are fast emerging towards product driven researchand technological developments on the international forum. In this direction the design andapplication of ER dampers for solution to vibration control problems with novel materials iscertainly an innovative challenge. This will lead to new areas of fluids and particles and theircombinations to create new technologies. There is also a potential for technology applicationsin established automotive to aerospace laboratories and industries.Specific courses required for project execution:1. name: Centre for High Impact Neuroscience and Translational Applications, TCG CREST
project_details:
Title: Video analysis for animal behavior
Description: The aim of this project is to analyze video recordings of insects navigating an open arena under various experimental conditions. This requires training a deep neural network model to identify specific types of insects in images and segment them (instance segmentation). The candidate will plug the trained model into the Argos toolkit and use it to extract the tracks of the insects over time. They will then analyze these tracks to identify the strategies that that insects use for navigation. They will also use pose estimation tools like DeepLabCut to find finer details of the posture of the animals and classify their behavior. Finally, they will standardize and document these methods to develop a workflow for analyzing animal behavior from video recordings.Skill sets: Python programming, Scientific Python (scipy, scikits-learn, Pytorch), AI/ML, Image processingExpected learning: Software engineering, AI/ML, Computer VisionSpecific courses required for project execution: Computer Programming, Introductory AI/ML, Image Processing, Computer Vision

name: Digibuks LLP  Noida
project_details:
Title: Tax & Compliance Accelerator: Real-World Income Tax, GST & Business Compliance - Noida,Digital Brand Builder: Scaling Digibuks’ Social Media Presence – Mumbai
Description:This project provides interns with hands-on exposure to real client engagements in the areas of IncomeTax, GST, and Business Compliance. Interns will work closely with professionals and interact directlywith clients to understand their financial profile, compliance requirements, and potential tax planningopportunities.Key responsibilities will include: Understanding client financial and business profiles Collecting and organizing financial data and documentation Assisting in preparation and filing of Income Tax Returns and GST returns Identifying opportunities for tax planning and compliance optimization Supporting bookkeeping activities and financial data organization Assisting in ROC compliance and basic corporate filings where applicableInterns will gain exposure to real client scenarios across individuals, professionals, and businesses,providing a practical understanding of the Indian taxation ecosystem.Skill sets:Basic understanding of Income Tax and GST laws Analytical and problem-solving skills Strong communication and client interaction skillsAttention to detail and documentation skills Basic accounting and bookkeeping knowledge Proficiency in Excel or accounting software is an added advantageExpected learning:By the end of the project, interns will: Understand practical aspects of Income Tax and GST compliance Gain experience in client interaction and financial data analysis Learn the process of tax return preparation and filingUnderstand basic bookkeeping and corporate compliance processesDevelop professional communication and consulting skillsSpecific courses required for project execution:Financial AccountingDirect TaxationIndirect Taxation (GST)Corporate Law / Business LawBasic Excel / Data Analysis,Description:This project focuses on building a strong digital identity and social media presence for Digibuks and itsgroup companies. Interns will design and execute a strategic social media plan to enhance brandvisibility, audience engagement, and digital reach.Key responsibilities will include: Creating a social media avatar and brand voice for Digibuks Designing a structured content pipeline across platformsDeveloping engaging content including posts, short videos, infographics, and thoughtleadership posts Managing and optimizing social media platforms such as LinkedIn, Instagram, and othersMonitoring engagement metrics including views, reach, impressions, and follower growth Suggesting innovative digital campaigns to increase brand visibilityInterns will get an opportunity to work at the intersection of finance, branding, and digital marketing,helping position Digibuks as a thought leader in the accounting and compliance ecosystem.Skill sets:Social media management and digital marketing understandingContent writing and storytelling ability Creativity and visual communication skills Basic design skills (Canva or similar tools preferred) Data analysis and performance tracking Marketing and brand strategy thinkingExpected learning:By the end of the project, interns will: Learn end-to-end social media strategy creation Understand brand positioning for professional services firms Develop skills in content marketing and audience engagement Gain exposure to digital analytics and growth metrics Build practical experience in executing real digital marketing campaignsSpecific courses required for project execution: Marketing Management Digital Marketing / Social Media Marketing Business Communication Brand ManagementData Analytics / Marketing Analytics (preferred)

name: Four Kites
project_details:
Yard Automation Solution,High Availability & Scalability Initiatives,Enhance In-House Automation Tool,Intelligent Bug Resolver,Exhaustive Unit Test Coverage,Mobile App Automation
Project DescriptionThe Yard Management Solution relies heavily on manual, repetitive tasks — tracking trailermovements, updating statuses, and coordinating check-ins — which are time-consuming anderror-prone. This project challenges intern engineers to build an AI-powered Yard AutomationSolution that eliminates these inefficiencies and streamlines end-to-end yard workflows. Internswill work on real-world logistics problems, designing intelligent systems that reduce humanintervention while improving speed and accuracy across yard operations.Opportunity to LearnInterns will gain hands-on experience with leading AI frameworks and tools used in productionenvironments. A standout learning opportunity is deep exposure to LOFT, FourKites' proprietaryworkflow orchestration platform built exclusively in-house, which powers mission-criticalautomation across the product. Engineers will also develop skills in AI pipeline design,real-world problem solving, and deploying intelligent automation in a fast-paced logisticstechnology environment.,Project DescriptionThe Yard Management Solution is a mission-critical operational application that must remainhighly available and performant at all times. The system encompasses complex business logicand a sophisticated data model that, if not optimized, can become a bottleneck as usage scales.This project tasks intern engineers with identifying and addressing performance gaps — frominefficient database queries to suboptimal code paths — to ensure the system meets the uptimeand scalability demands of real-world yard operations.Opportunity to LearnInterns will develop strong SQL skills by working on complex query optimization, indexingstrategies, and data model design in a production-grade environment. They will also learn howto write efficient, performance-aware code and gain exposure to performance testingframeworks used to benchmark and validate system improvements. This project buildsfoundational engineering skills that are critical for anyone looking to build and maintain highlyavailable, enterprise-scale systems.,Project DescriptionFourKites has built an in-house automation tool designed to functionally test newly developedfeatures of the Yard Management Solution. However, the tool's current accuracy falls below thethreshold required for reliable CI/CD integration. This project tasks intern engineers withimproving the tool's test accuracy to 95% or above, making it robust enough for seamlessintegration into the development lifecycle and reducing the manual QA burden acrossengineering teams.Opportunity to LearnInterns will deep dive into advanced AI techniques including prompt fine-tuning,Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), and model optimization strategies. They will analyzefailure patterns, iterate on model behavior, and apply real-world evaluation methodologies tomeasure and improve performance. This project provides a rich learning curve in promptengineering, model accuracy improvement, and AI-driven quality assurance — skills that arehighly sought after in the modern AI engineering landscape.,Project DescriptionSoftware bugs are inevitable, but the time engineers spend diagnosing and fixing them doesn'thave to be. FourKites is building an intelligent bug resolution system powered by Claude Codethat automatically fixes code defects in the Yard Management Solution based on root causeanalyses (RCA) generated by an existing RCA Bot. Interns will design and build agents thatinterpret RCA reports, understand codebase context, and generate precise, validated code fixesautonomously.Opportunity to LearnInterns will gain hands-on experience at the intersection of AI and software engineering —working with agentic AI systems, code generation models, and LLM-powered pipelines. Theywill also learn how to integrate AI workflows with version control systems and implementautomated validation strategies to ensure fix quality. This project offers a high-impact, practicalintroduction to autonomous AI agents and real-world code automation — one of the mostexciting frontiers in AI engineering today.,Project DescriptionThe Yard Management Solution operates across a complex distributed architecture spanning15+ microservices, each requiring rigorous validation to ensure data integrity and systemreliability. This project challenges intern engineers to build exhaustive unit test coverage acrossall services — going beyond happy-path scenarios to include comprehensive negative flowvalidations that ensure the system correctly rejects bad data, malformed requests, andunexpected edge cases. Maintaining this quality bar is essential to operating at enterprise scale.Opportunity to LearnInterns will leverage Claude Code and other modern AI tools to accelerate test generation andsharpen their prompting skills in a practical, high-stakes engineering context. They will gain adeep understanding of how enterprise-grade systems maintain quality across distributedservices, learn best practices in test design for both positive and negative flows, and develop anappreciation for how robust validation layers protect system integrity. This project is an excellentfoundation for any engineer looking to build reliable, production-ready software.,Project DescriptionFourKites' mobile application for the Yard Management Solution serves thousands of users inthe field, making reliability and quality non-negotiable. Currently, the mobile app lacksautomated test coverage, creating risk with every new release. This project tasks internengineers with implementing a comprehensive mobile automation framework from the groundup — covering critical user journeys, edge cases, and regression scenarios to ensure aseamless experience across devices and platforms.Opportunity to LearnInterns will get hands-on experience with Flutter, one of the most widely adopted cross-platformmobile development frameworks, and Patrol, a powerful open-source tool purpose-built forFlutter app automation. They will learn how to design scalable automation architectures formobile applications, write maintainable test suites, and integrate them into a CI/CD pipeline.This project offers a strong learning curve in mobile development, test automation, and moderncross-platform engineering practices.

name: GMDC Science and Research Centre
project_details:
Title: Development of Coal and Lignite additives to improve their combustions, efficiency, calorific value, and reduction in pollution levels.
Description: Coal and Lignite are important fossil fuels, used for electric power generation, besides use in the boiler of industries. They are the most demanded energy sources, and their consumption has been increasing. However, there is a need to improve coal combustion performance to enhance the efficiency of furnace and boiler. Addition of additives to both fuels is an option and has led to focus by researchers on developing additive. Such additives can effectively improve the combustion performance of the coal and lignite. A study on the subject would contribute to providing information about the impact of additives on combustion properties, calorific value and a hybrid technology that may use additives to modify the coal/lignite surface. Thus, the objective of this study is to relate the effect of additives on utilization and combustion performances of both the fuels.Improved efficiency and reduced emissions from coal/lignite power stations may lead to development of present and future efficient coal/lignite combustion technologies. Upgrading coal, reducing emission of NOx, SO2 and particulate matter, and corrosion are some of the factors in the development of efficient coal technologies. Such additive could be a finely milled 2 multi-oxide/carbonate additives when mixed with the coal/lignite without the need to change the boiler installation has proven to increase the boiler efficiency, flame temperature with reduction in corrosion, NOx and particulate matter (dust) emissions. The process may be developed through bench, pilot and full scale. It would be tested at lab scale and actual operation.Skill sets: Instrumentation / Chemical AnalysisExpected learning: Understanding of Combustion Properties, Additive Development and applicationSpecific courses required for project execution: Chemical Engineering / Materials Science & Engineering / Chemistry

name: GMDC Science and Research Centre
project_details:
Title: Identifying Indicator minerals and Pathfinder elements in mineral exploration.
Description: The traditional approach in carrying out mineral exploration studies included collection of samples including rock drills cores and analyzing them in the laboratory to generate data. This used to be costly, time consuming, and laborious for mine areas with difficult terrain. With the advent of latest technologies, especially field portable analytical instruments viz. portable visible and near-infrared spectrophotometers, gamma-ray spectrometer, LED fluorimeter, pXRF, pXRD, pLIBS, and muRaman spectrometer, their on-site use has increased in mineral exploration. Furthermore, use of these instruments on site do help in quick, real-time, non-destructive, and cost-effective identification and determination of indicator minerals and pathfinder elements in rock and ores, in soil sediment, and in water samples. Such portable analytical instruments determine accurate chemical and mineralogical data during the field visits with virtually no or minimum laboratory analysis, thus bypassing the need for sample collection. The information so obtained helps in decision making in carrying out field work as also during drilling operations in mineral exploration.In our research use of such portable devices would be made in carrying out exploration studies for Gold (Au), Silver (Ag), Rubidium (Rb), Hafnium (Hf), Zirconium (Zr), and a group of 17 Rare Earth Elements (REE) in Ambaji mines.Skill sets: Instrumentation / Chemical AnalysisExpected learning: a. It would help identify the indicator minerals and path finder elements, making the mining activity more focused. b. It would promote sustainable mining including underground mining. c. It would promote collaboration with the technology providers across the world. d. It would lead to exchange of experts on the issue from Universities and Institutes of repute across the world, including collaboration with such organizations.

name: GMDC Science and Research Centre
project_details:
Title: Establishment of Snake Park at Kadipani, Gujarat
Description: It is proposed to establish a Snake Park at Kadipani in Kawant Taluka in Chhota Udepur District in Gujarat (India). The park is envisaged to be run as a means of education of the public on snakes, while ensuring conservation of large number of snakes found in the region. The snake park would be located on GMDC land admeasuring around 2 acres of land on the located in the NE corner. The place is approximately 13 km away from Kawant and around 240 km from Ahmedabad. It is well served by the public transport system. It is proposed to set up the Snake Park under the supervision and guidance of Project Manager at Kadipani assisted by the suitable staff.Skill sets: Instrumentation / Chemical Analysis / BiochemistryExpected learning: a. Learning about conservation of various species of snake found at Kadipani and in adjacent areas. b. Learning to conduct surveys to assess their status and distribution in the area.Specific courses required for project execution: Chemical Engineering / Chemistry / Biological Sciences

Title: Graph Algorithmic Optimization for Spatial Layouts,Title: Software Development in the Web Application and Desktop Application,Title: Software Development in the Backend Application,Title: Marketing and content development,Title: Design and video making,Title: Web development
Description: This project focuses on the development and implementation of advanced graphalgorithms for spatial layout optimization. Students will delve into creating algorithms for taskssuch as minimal dimensioning on floor plan layouts, handling polygonal floor plans, andgenerating 3D layouts with precise dimensions. The emphasis is on practical applications ofgraph theory to solve real-world spatial optimization challenges.Skill sets: Python Programming with common libraries like numpy, tkinter, networkx, Object-Oriented Programming (OOP), Optimization Techniques, Graph Theory,Expected learning: Participants in this project can expect to:Develop proficiency in Python and OOP for algorithmic implementation.Gain a deep understanding of optimization techniques for spatial layout challenges.Apply graph theory concepts to solve real-world problems in spatial design.Learn how to write industry-level algorithms with a focus on efficiency and scalability.Specific courses required for project execution: Object-Oriented Programming, Graph andNetworks, Discrete Structures for Computer Science, Optimization,Description: This software development project aims to create a robust backend architecture toscale and support the graph algorithms developed in the previous project. The focus is onbuilding a scalable infrastructure that efficiently handles spatial layout optimizationcomputations. Additionally, participants will design and implement a user-friendly web interfaceaccessible to all clients. The project also includes the development of a desktop application,providing clients with a seamless end-to-end experience in interacting with the spatialoptimization algorithms.Skill sets: Python (Flask, Numpy), Javascript (React, D3, Redux, Electron)Expected learning: Participants engaging in this project will gain expertise in:Building scalable backend architectures to handle algorithmic computations efficiently.Designing and developing user-friendly web interfaces for clients to interact with the spatialoptimization system.Creating desktop applications to provide a comprehensive end-to-end experience for clients.Integrating graph algorithms into a software environment.Understanding the challenges and best practices of scaling cloud systems.Specific courses required for project execution: Computer Programming,Description: This backend development project looks for Backend Development students,mainly in Python/Django. Postgres.Familiarity with containerization tools like Docker, and Linux.Experience with RESTful API design and implementation.Familiarity with version control systems (e.g., Git) and agile name: Inc42 Media
project_details:
Market Research & Customer Discovery,Product Improvement & Process Optimization
Description: The intern will conduct structured market research to understand user needs, industry dynamics,and existing gaps in the Datalabs product offering. This will involve interacting with existing customers, potentialusers, and internal stakeholders through calls, emails, and surveys. The intern will analyse feedback to identifypatterns in user expectations, product usage, and unmet needs. During this phase, the intern will also gainexposure to cross-functional business operations including product management, design, engineering, dataanalysis, and marketing to understand how a technology product is developed and scaled. Insights gatheredduring this phase will be documented as actionable research findings that inform product improvements.Skill sets: Market research, user interviews, business communication, analytical thinking, qualitativeresearch, stakeholder interaction, data analysis, documentation and presentation.Expected learning: Understanding of B2B SaaS ecosystems, practical exposure to customer discovery processes,fundamentals of product management, insights into cross-functional collaboration in a technology startup, andmethods of translating customer feedback into product insights.Specific courses required for project execution: Marketing Research, Business Communication, ProductManagement fundamentals, Data Analysis.,Description: Based on insights derived from the market research phase, the intern will work on identifyingopportunities to improve the Datalabs platform, particularly around its primary activation point of leadgeneration for investors and sales & marketing teams. The intern will evaluate existing workflows, data qualityprocesses, and user experience gaps, and recommend improvements that can enhance product value. This mayinclude refining data processes, suggesting feature improvements, improving research workflows, or proposingnew approaches that connect real-world user feedback with product development priorities. The intern willpresent a final set of actionable recommendations supported by research insights and practical feasibilityconsiderations.Skill sets: Problem solving, product thinking, process improvement, data interpretation, strategic analysis,documentation, and presentation skills.Expected learning: Hands-on experience in product improvement initiatives, understanding how user insightstranslate into product decisions, exposure to startup decision-making processes, and practical knowledge ofbuilding data-driven products for business users.Specific courses required for project execution: Product Management, Product Analytics, UI/UX Design,Growth Hacking, Software Engineering, Data Engineering.

name: Induma Infratech pvt ltd.
project_details:
Real Estate Market Intelligence & Seller Discovery,AI Property Advisor for Rapid Customer Decision-Making
Title: Real Estate Market Intelligence & Seller DiscoveryDescription: The intern will conduct structured market research to identify pain points in property disposition and management. Primary focus is on understanding the friction points for property owners, developers, and institutional sellers. This involves direct engagement with leads, existing clients, and brokerage partners via calls, site visit follow-ups, and targeted surveys. The intern will analyze qualitative data to identify patterns in pricing expectations, time-on-market sensitivities, and service gaps in current property management offerings. Exposure includes cross-functional alignment with Sales, Legal/Compliance, CRM Operations, and Marketing to understand the lifecycle of a real estate asset from listing to closing. Findings will be synthesized into actionable intelligence to refine lead conversion scripts and service-level agreements (SLAs).Skill sets: Market analysis, business communication, CRM data entry/analysis, qualitative research, stakeholder management, professional documentation, and presentation.Expected learning: Understanding of the PropTech and property management ecosystem, practical exposure to the real estate sales funnel, fundamentals of asset management, insights into cross-functional coordination between legal and sales teams, and methods of translating seller frustration into value-added service features.Specific courses required for project execution: Real Estate Principles/Marketing, Business Communication, Data Analysis, Sales & Negotiation Fundamentals.,Title: AI Property Advisor for Rapid Customer Decision-MakingDescription: The intern will develop and integrate a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) "Smart Search" assistant for the company’s website. The goal is to replace complex filters with a simple chat interface where users describe their needs in plain English (e.g., "I'm looking for a quiet 2-bedroom apartment with low maintenance costs near the metro"). The intern will organize a database of property listings, amenities, and neighborhood highlights. The AI model will then "retrieve" the best matches and explain why they fit the user's specific lifestyle or budget. This shortens the decision-making funnel by providing immediate, personalized suggestions, significantly reducing the time a user spends browsing and increasing the likelihood of them booking a site visit.Skill sets: Basic AI prompt design, digital customer journey mapping, data organization (Excel/Google Sheets), clear business writing, and user feedback collection.Expected learning: How to use AI to solve real-world customer frustrations, basic understanding of how "Search" is evolving into "Advice," and how to simplify complex property data into easy-to-read recommendations.Specific courses required for project execution: AI Fundamentals, User Experience (UX), Digital Marketing, Data Organization

The intern will analyze qualitative data to identify patterns in pricing expectations, time-on-market sensitivities, and service gaps in current property management offerings. Exposure includes cross-functional alignment with Sales, Legal/Compliance, CRM Operations, and Marketing to understand the lifecycle of a real estate asset from listing to closing. Findings will be synthesized into actionable intelligence to refine lead conversion scripts and service-level agreements (SLAs).Skill sets: Market analysis, business communication, CRM data entry/analysis, qualitative research, stakeholder management, professional documentation, and presentation.Expected learning: Understanding of the PropTech and property management ecosystem, practical exposure to the real estate sales funnel, fundamentals of asset management, insights into cross-functional coordination between legal and sales teams, and methods of translating seller frustration into value-added service features.Specific courses required for project execution: Real Estate Principles/Marketing, Business Communication, Data Analysis, Sales & Negotiation Fundamentals.,Title: AI Property Advisor for Rapid Customer Decision-MakingDescription: The intern will develop and integrate a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) "Smart Search" assistant for the company’s website. The goal is to replace complex filters with a simple chat interface where users describe their needs in plain English (e.g., "I'm looking for a quiet 2-bedroom apartment with low maintenance costs near the metro"). The intern will organize a database of property listings, amenities, and neighborhood highlights. The AI model will then "retrieve" the best matches and explain why they fit the user's specific lifestyle or budget. This shortens the decision-making funnel by providing immediate, personalized suggestions, significantly reducing the time a user spends browsing and increasing the likelihood of them booking a site visit.Skill sets: Basic AI prompt design, digital customer journey mapping, data organization (Excel/Google Sheets), clear business writing, and user feedback collection.Expected learning: How to use AI to solve real-world customer frustrations, basic understanding of how "Search" is evolving into "Advice," and how to simplify complex property data into easy-to-read recommendations.Specific courses required for project execution: AI Fundamentals, User Experience (UX), Digital Marketing, Data Organization
raw_row.Title: Real Estate Market Intelligence & Seller Discovery,AI Property Advisor for Rapid Customer Decision-Making

Description: Conventional steering system on a boat œnsists of a mechaniœl or hydraulic actuator, connected to a helm through a mechaniœl cable or hydraulic hoses. When the steeûng wheel œnnected to the helm is manually turned, the actuator moves and rotates the engine. The aim of this project is to replace this system with a DE or Servo motor driven achJator œntrolled by an electronic helm unit. The main advantage of this system would be that the helm unit can then accept electronic control from a navigation computer in addition to manual control.Skill sels: Knowledge of 3D CAD Software, Electronic DesignExpected Learning: The student will leam how to design, simulate and manufacture a product from conœpt to actual working. Specific courses required for project executîon: CAD, Basic Electrical and Mechanical Design  ,	Descripfion: The aim of this project is to develop a Navigation Controller which interfaces with our existing electûc drive system to ultimately' have the œpability to œmpletely navigate a boat along a programmed path. The initial steps would be to introduce features such as position holding without the use of a mechaniœl anchor, basic boat steedng control and movement along a programmed path. name: LOHUM Cleantech Private Limited
project_details:
Process development for Silver nanoparticles based conducting ink for electronic application
Description: the synthesis of silver nanoparticles and their formulation into a stable conductive ink for printed electronics applications. The study focuses on optimizing nanoparticle size, dispersion stability, ink rheology, and curing conditions to achieve high electrical conductivity and strong substrate adhesion. The developed ink will be tested by printing conductive patterns and evaluating their electrical, mechanical, and microstructural performance.Skill sets: Chemistry, Material science, Chemical EngineeringExpected learning: • Understanding the principles and methods of silver nanoparticle synthesis using chemical reduction techniques• Knowledge of surface stabilization mechanisms to prevent nanoparticle agglomeration• Skills in preparing stable nanoparticle dispersions with controlled particle size and rheology• Hands-on experience in formulating conductive inks and printing them on electronic substrates• Ability to evaluate electrical performance and practical applications of conductive inks in printed electronicsSpecific courses required for project execution: Chemistry, Material Science, Chemical Engineering

name: Maritime Research Center, Foundation for Underwater Domain Awareeness
project_details:
Potential Fishing Zones,Fisheries and Aquaculture in Andaman and Nicobar Island,Modelling & Simulation for Brahmaputra Coast/ Konkan Coast,Ocean Temperature, Sea Surface Temperature and Wind  Modelling for EEZ,Sediment Transport Modelling for Brahmaputra River,Acoustic (Ultrasonic) Antifouling on Ship Hulls in Tropical Regions,Satellite-Based Sediment Load Estimation and Sediment Classification,Safeguarding Undersea Cables for India’s Strategic Security,Neural Network for Estimation of Transmission Loss in Indian  Ocean Region,Dark Ship Detection & Classification using SAR Imagery in the Indian Ocean Region,Marine Spatial Planning for Rivers using RIS / AIS Data,Habitat Suitability Model,Water Resource Management for the Riverine System of Brahmaputra,Groundwater Management in India, Machine-Learning-based model for  predicting Groundwater,Multiparameter-sensor-based-water-quality-monitoring
Description: Identifying areas with high fish aggregation, known as Potential FishingZones(PFZ), is crucial for optimizing fishing efforts and ensuring sustainable fisheries.Traditionally, fishermen spend significant time and resources searching forproductive fishing grounds, often leading to increased fuel consumption,operational costs, and pressure on fish stocks.. The lack of timely and accurateinformation on fish distribution exacerbates these issues, affecting both thelivelihoods of fishing communities and the sustainability of marine resources.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description: In 2025, the government emphasized a cluster-based approach forfisheries and aquaculture development. The Andaman & Nicobar Islandshave been notified as a special tuna cluster under the Pradhan MantriMatsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY), aiming to organize and modernize thesector, attract investments, and generate economies of scale.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description: The Brahmaputra River supports rich biodiversity and sustains millionsthrough fisheries but faces challenges like floods. The Konkan Coast drives India’smarine economy but needs resilient models for cyclones and sustainable practices.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description: Monitoring ocean temperature, sea surface temperature, and wind inthe EEZ is important because it helps protect marine ecosystems, supportssustainable fishing, and manages valuable natural resources. Accurate climatemodelling in the EEZ is crucial for disaster preparedness, energy production, safenavigation, and boosting the coastal economy.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description: This project aims to develop a comprehensivemodel and simulation framework to analyse sediment transport dynamicswithin the Brahmaputra River. Understanding sediment transport patternsis critical for river management, flood risk mitigation, navigation, andecological sustainability. It will focus on identifying temporal and spatialvariations in sediment load and their implications on river morphology andsurrounding environments.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description: Biofouling-the accumulation of marine organisms such as algae,barnacles, and mussels on ship hulls is a persistent challenge, especially in tropicalwaters where high biodiversity and warm temperatures accelerate foulingrates.Traditional antifouling methods often rely on toxic coatings, which poseenvironmental risks. Ultrasonic (acoustic) antifouling offers a non-toxic, proactivealternative, but its effectiveness and operational parameters must be optimised forthe unique conditions of tropical marine environments.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description: Traditional hydrographic and field-based sediment surveys, whileaccurate,are resource-intensive and often lack temporal and spatial coverage.Satellite remote sensing offers a cost-effective, rapid, and synoptic alternative forquantifying sediment load and classifying sediment types over large andinaccessible areas.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description: India’s undersea cables form the backbone of its digital andstrategic communications, carrying over 99% of international data traffic. However,these cables are increasingly vulnerable to accidental damage, sabotage, andgeopolitical threats, especially given their concentration at a few landing points andIndia’s current reliance on foreign repair and installation capacity. Ensuring thesecurity, resilience, and rapid restoration of these critical assets is essential fornational security,economic stability, and India’s ambitions to become a globaldigital hub.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description: The existing state-of-art algorithms for estimation of soundtransmission loss in the Indian ocean region make use of mathematical modelswhile making use of the sound speed profile of the region. For E.g.: ParabolicEquation based Range Dependent Acoustic Propagation Model for Indian OceanRegion. However, these models provide a solution of time complexity O(n), which isextremely large when computed over a large region (Eg whole of Arabian sea). Thisproject therefore pitches the use of Neural Network for estimation of soundtransmission loss trained for the region of Arabian Sea and Bay of Bengalsurrounding the Indian Subcontinent, such that the computational time is reduced.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language, understanding of ML concepts.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description: Illegal, Unreported, and Unregulated (IUU) fishing, smuggling, andmaritime boundary violations often involve vessels that disable their AIS (AutomaticIdentification System) to avoid detection—commonly referred to as “Dark Ships.”Traditional AIS-based tracking becomes inactive in such cases. However, SyntheticAperture Radar (SAR) satellite imagery—capable of all-weather, day-nightmonitoring—can detect the physical presence of vessels regardless of AIS status.By cross-referencing SAR-derived ship detections with AIS-transmitting ships in thesame spatiotemporal window, it is possible to identify and flag vessels operating inthe dark.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description: The existing shipping radiated noise estimation tool of MRC (SRN),aims at performing marine spatial planning for open ocean of Arabian sea and bayof Bengal, however a higher-resolution spatial planning of river systems involves amyriad of applications in Inland Waterway Development (Port & Terminal SiteSelection, Identification of congestion zones, flood warning etc). This project aimsto create a proof of concept – marine spatial planning – for a pilot (BrahmaputraRiver) in India aimed at Vessel noise Heat Map highlighting river segments with hightra@ic + high acoustic load.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description: The Shipping Radiated Noise (SRN) Tool currently supportshigh-resolution ambient noise mapping for open ocean regions such as the ArabianSea and Bay of Bengal. However, ecological interpretation of these noise maps ismissing—particularly in the context of marine species’ habitat suitability undervarying acoustic and oceanographic Conditions. This project aims to develop andintegrate a Habitat Suitability Model (HSM) into the SRN tool by combining theambient noise data with other biophysical parameters (e.g., bathymetry,temperature, chlorophyll, salinity, turbidity) to evaluate species-specific ecologicalcompatibility across different oceanic regionsSkill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description: This project focuses on integrated water resource management for theBrahmaputra riverine system, addressing hydrology, sediment dynamics, flood risk,and ecosystem sustainability. It supports data-driven planning and policydevelopment to ensure balanced utilization, resilience, and long-term environmentalsecurity.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description:This project develops a machine learning–based model to predict groundwateravailability and trends across India, supporting sustainable water resourcemanagement. By integrating hydrological, climatic, and geospatial data, it enablesdata-driven decision-making for groundwater conservation and policy planning.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language, understanding of ML concepts.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description:This project develops a multiparameter sensor-based system for real-time waterquality monitoring by measuring key indicators such as pH, dissolved oxygen,turbidity, temperature, and conductivity. It enables continuous assessment, earlypollution detection, and data-driven management of aquatic environments.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.

It will focus on identifying temporal and spatialvariations in sediment load and their implications on river morphology andsurrounding environments.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description: Biofouling-the accumulation of marine organisms such as algae,barnacles, and mussels on ship hulls is a persistent challenge, especially in tropicalwaters where high biodiversity and warm temperatures accelerate foulingrates.Traditional antifouling methods often rely on toxic coatings, which poseenvironmental risks. Ultrasonic (acoustic) antifouling offers a non-toxic, proactivealternative, but its effectiveness and operational parameters must be optimised forthe unique conditions of tropical marine environments.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description: Traditional hydrographic and field-based sediment surveys, whileaccurate,are resource-intensive and often lack temporal and spatial coverage.Satellite remote sensing offers a cost-effective, rapid, and synoptic alternative forquantifying sediment load and classifying sediment types over large andinaccessible areas.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description: India’s undersea cables form the backbone of its digital andstrategic communications, carrying over 99% of international data traffic. However,these cables are increasingly vulnerable to accidental damage, sabotage, andgeopolitical threats, especially given their concentration at a few landing points andIndia’s current reliance on foreign repair and installation capacity. Ensuring thesecurity, resilience, and rapid restoration of these critical assets is essential fornational security,economic stability, and India’s ambitions to become a globaldigital hub.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description: The existing state-of-art algorithms for estimation of soundtransmission loss in the Indian ocean region make use of mathematical modelswhile making use of the sound speed profile of the region. For E.g.: ParabolicEquation based Range Dependent Acoustic Propagation Model for Indian OceanRegion. However, these models provide a solution of time complexity O(n), which isextremely large when computed over a large region (Eg whole of Arabian sea). Thisproject therefore pitches the use of Neural Network for estimation of soundtransmission loss trained for the region of Arabian Sea and Bay of Bengalsurrounding the Indian Subcontinent, such that the computational time is reduced.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language, understanding of ML concepts.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description: Illegal, Unreported, and Unregulated (IUU) fishing, smuggling, andmaritime boundary violations often involve vessels that disable their AIS (AutomaticIdentification System) to avoid detection—commonly referred to as “Dark Ships.”Traditional AIS-based tracking becomes inactive in such cases. However, SyntheticAperture Radar (SAR) satellite imagery—capable of all-weather, day-nightmonitoring—can detect the physical presence of vessels regardless of AIS status.By cross-referencing SAR-derived ship detections with AIS-transmitting ships in thesame spatiotemporal window, it is possible to identify and flag vessels operating inthe dark.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description: The existing shipping radiated noise estimation tool of MRC (SRN),aims at performing marine spatial planning for open ocean of Arabian sea and bayof Bengal, however a higher-resolution spatial planning of river systems involves amyriad of applications in Inland Waterway Development (Port & Terminal SiteSelection, Identification of congestion zones, flood warning etc). This project aimsto create a proof of concept – marine spatial planning – for a pilot (BrahmaputraRiver) in India aimed at Vessel noise Heat Map highlighting river segments with hightra@ic + high acoustic load.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description: The Shipping Radiated Noise (SRN) Tool currently supportshigh-resolution ambient noise mapping for open ocean regions such as the ArabianSea and Bay of Bengal. However, ecological interpretation of these noise maps ismissing—particularly in the context of marine species’ habitat suitability undervarying acoustic and oceanographic Conditions. This project aims to develop andintegrate a Habitat Suitability Model (HSM) into the SRN tool by combining theambient noise data with other biophysical parameters (e.g., bathymetry,temperature, chlorophyll, salinity, turbidity) to evaluate species-specific ecologicalcompatibility across different oceanic regionsSkill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description: This project focuses on integrated water resource management for theBrahmaputra riverine system, addressing hydrology, sediment dynamics, flood risk,and ecosystem sustainability. It supports data-driven planning and policydevelopment to ensure balanced utilization, resilience, and long-term environmentalsecurity.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description:This project develops a machine learning–based model to predict groundwateravailability and trends across India, supporting sustainable water resourcemanagement. By integrating hydrological, climatic, and geospatial data, it enablesdata-driven decision-making for groundwater conservation and policy planning.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language, understanding of ML concepts.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.,Description:This project develops a multiparameter sensor-based system for real-time waterquality monitoring by measuring key indicators such as pH, dissolved oxygen,turbidity, temperature, and conductivity. It enables continuous assessment, earlypollution detection, and data-driven management of aquatic environments.Skill sets: STEM understanding, ability to perform modelling & simulations usingprogramming language.Expected learning: Real world problem solvingSpecific courses required for project execution: No prior experience expected,initial training and mentorship will be provided.

Title: DolphinCX - LLM Based Voice Virtual Agent,Title: DolphinCX - AI Knowledge Assistant & RAG Pipeline,Title: DolphinCX - Inbound & Outbound Calling and Campaign Automation,Title: DolphinCX - Real-Time Media Streaming & Recording,Title: DolphinCX - ERP & CRM Integration
Description: The project involves building of voice virtual agents leveraging Large Language Models(LLMs) such as GPT, Llama, and in-house fine-tuned models, along with technologies like ASR/STT,TTS, and frameworks like TensorFlow, PyTorch, in-house frameworks etc., enabling autonomoushandling of inbound and outbound customer calls without human intervention. The project also involvesbuilding domain-specific fine-tuned models trained on conversation data, as RAG-based approachesintroduce latency that is unsuitable for real-time conversational AI, making fine-tuned models a moreeffective choice for low-latency voice interactions.Skill sets: Large Language Models (LLMs) - GPT, Llama, fine-tuning; Automatic Speech Recognition(ASR) / Speech-to-Text (STT); Text-to-Speech (TTS) synthesis; Deep learning frameworks -TensorFlow, PyTorch; Python programming; Conversational AI design and dialogue management;Model evaluation and benchmarkingExpected learning: Hands-on experience building and deploying production-grade voice AI agents;Understanding of LLM fine-tuning pipelines and training workflows; Exposure to real-time speechprocessing and low-latency AI system design; Practical knowledge of conversational flow design andintent handling; Understanding of trade-offs between RAG and fine-tuned models in productionSpecific courses required for project execution: Deep Learning Specialization (Coursera / AndrewNg); Natural Language Processing with Transformers; Speech and Language Processing (Stanford /Jurafsky & Martin); LLM Fine-Tuning and RLHF (Hugging Face courses); Python for AI/ML,Description: The project involves building of a RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) based AIKnowledge Assistant leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs), vector databases, anddocument parsing frameworks to enable intelligent, context-aware question answering overenterprise knowledge bases and documents.Skill sets: Large Language Models (LLMs) and prompt engineering; Retrieval-AugmentedGeneration (RAG) architecture; Vector databases (Milvus, Pinecone, FAISS); Document parsingand ingestion (PDF, DOCX, PPTX); Python programming; REST API development; Async taskprocessingExpected learning: Hands-on experience designing and deploying RAG pipelines;Understanding of vector embeddings and semantic search; Exposure to enterprise documentprocessing workflows; Practical knowledge of LLM orchestration and multi-model routing;Understanding of caching strategies for AI workloadsSpecific courses required for project execution: LangChain & LlamaIndex for RAG Applications;Vector Databases and Embeddings (DeepLearning.AI); Python for Data Engineering; Building AIApplications with OpenAI API; Database fundamentals,Description: The project involves development of an Inbound and Outbound Calling andCampaign Automation platform built in Golang and Python following a distributed microservicesarchitecture, leveraging cloud-based telephony, CRM integrations, and workflow automationframeworks. The platform also involves building and deploying fine-tuned LLM models for intentdetection and call routing, enabling businesses to handle inbound customer calls intelligentlyand run large-scale outbound outreach campaigns at scale.Skill sets: Golang and Python programming; Distributed microservices architecture; SIP / VoIPtelephony protocols; REST API design and development; Message queues and asyncprocessing (Redis, Celery); CRM integration and workflow automation; LLM fine-tuning forclassification and intent detectionExpected learning: Hands-on experience building distributed backend services in Golang andPython; Understanding of VoIP and telephony system design; Exposure to large-scale campaignmanagement and scheduling systems; Practical knowledge of microservices patterns - servicediscovery, load balancing; Understanding of LLM-based intent detection in call center workflowsSpecific courses required for project execution: Go Programming Language (Golang) - Beginnerto Advanced; Microservices Architecture (Udemy / Coursera); Distributed Systemsfundamentals; REST API Design and Development; Cloud Computing fundamentals (AWS / GCP),Description: The project involves development of a Real-Time Media Streaming and Recordinginfrastructure leveraging SFU (Selective Forwarding Unit) architecture, RTP/SRTP protocols,and cloud-based post-processing pipelines for high-quality call and meeting recording at scale.Skill sets: WebRTC and real-time media protocols (RTP, RTCP, SRTP); C/C++ and Goprogramming; SFU architecture and media server design; Cloud storage and post-processingpipelines; Network programming and packet-level media handling; Docker and container-baseddeploymentExpected learning: Hands-on experience with real-time audio/video media systems;Understanding of WebRTC internals and SFU design patterns; Exposure to low-level networkprogramming and RTP packet handling; Practical knowledge of media recording, transcoding,and archival workflows; Understanding of scalable cloud-based media processing pipelinesSpecific courses required for project execution: WebRTC for Beginners and Advanced; name: Pockvue Solutions Pvt. Ltd - Tech
project_details:
AI Story and Game Creator, AI-Powered Tutor Avatar for Personalized Learning,In-house AI-Based Online Exam Proctoring System, AI-Powered Adaptive Learning Platform
Title: AI Story and Game CreatorDescription: This project focuses on building an AI-powered platform that can generate interactive stories and game-like creative experiences using image and video generation models. The tool should allow users to create stories across predefined topics as well as custom topics of their choice. The platform will involve designing flows for story generation, visual generation, and user interaction, while integrating existing AI APIs and building a smooth end-to-end product experience. This project is ideal for someone excited by the intersection of creativity, AI, and product building, and who wants to work on a modern consumer-facing tool from concept to execution.Skill sets: Understanding of modern web technologies such as HTML, CSS, JavaScript, TypeScript, React, and frontend application architecture. Familiarity with backend fundamentals, APIs, databases, and cloud/infrastructure concepts, AI platforms, model APIs, and developer tools used for building generative AI products.Expected learning: React, AI API integration, infrastructure basics, GTM strategy, product design, and end-to-end product development.Specific courses required for project execution: CS course students required. Working knowledge of frontend development, React, API integration, JavaScript or TypeScript, basic backend concepts, and familiarity with AI tools/platforms,Title: AI-Powered Tutor Avatar for Personalized LearningDescription: Design and develop an AI-based tutor avatar capable of teaching specific topics using predefined scripts and answering basic student queries. The project involves exploring tools like conversational AI, text-to-speech, and simple avatar interfaces. The final solution should be integrable into the existing learning platform.Skill sets: Basic Python or JavaScript, Basic understanding of APIs, Frontend or Backend fundamentals, Willingness to learn new tools quickly.Expected learning: Basics of conversational AI and LLM usage, Working with APIs (AI, voice, etc.), Building simple interactive applications, Exposure to real-world product development.Specific courses required for project execution: CS course students required. Basic Programming (Python / JavaScript), Introductory course in AI or Data Science, Basic Web Development,Title: In-house AI-Based Online Exam Proctoring SystemDescription: Research and build a basic AI-driven proctoring system to monitor online exams and detect simple suspicious activities (e.g., multiple faces, absence from screen). The focus is on exploring computer vision tools and building a working prototype that can later be scaled.Skill sets: Basic Python, Interest in Computer Vision / AI, Familiarity with libraries (or willingness to learn) like OpenCV, Basic backend or scripting knowledge, Logical thinking and debugging skills.Expected learning: Basics of computer vision and image processing, Handling video/webcam input, Building simple AI-based monitoring systems, Rapid prototyping and experimentation.Specific courses required for project execution: CS course students required. Basic Programming (Python preferred), Intro to AI / Machine Learning, Data Structures basics (helpful).,Title: AI-Powered Adaptive Learning PlatformDescription: This project focuses on building an AI-powered learning platform that transforms raw study materials into interactive, multi-modal learning experiences. The tool should allow users to upload their own documents and notes, which are processed into a vector database and used as a grounded knowledge base for all AI outputs. From this knowledge base, the platform generates AI-narrated audio podcasts from the content, enables users to ask questions mid-playback and receive answers pulled directly from their materials, produces presentation slides summarising key concepts, and creates personalised lesson plans tailored to the user's goals and knowledge level.Skill sets: Understanding of modern web technologies such as HTML, CSS, JavaScript, TypeScript, React, and frontend application architecture. Familiarity with backend fundamentals, APIs, databases, and cloud/infrastructure concepts, working knowledge of AI platforms, model APIs.Expected learning: React, RAG pipeline design, vector database integration, AI API integration, infrastructure basics, product design.Specific courses required for project execution: : CS course students required. Working knowledge of frontend development, React, API integration, JavaScript or TypeScript, basic backend concepts, and familiarity with AI tools and platforms commonly used.

name: Regional Remote Sensing Center-North, NRSC, ISRO - Electronics
project_details:
Automated Satellite Image Coregistration for Multi Temporal Analysis
This project focuses on developing robust algorithms for accurate coregistration of multitemporal and multi sensor satellite imagery to sub pixel accuracy. The intern will work on implementing ans optimizing coregistration of coreristration techniques to align satellite images captured at different times for different sesnors.The project will involve handling challenges like varying illumination,seasonal changes and geometics distortions.Required skill & Traits: Strong progemming skills in python, understanding of computer vision and image processing concepts, familiarity with feature detection and matching alorithms ,ability to work with large geospatial datasets,self motivated and capable of independent research.Expected outcomes: Implementation of automated coregistration pipeline achieving sub pixel accuracy comparative analysis of different feature extraction methods for satellite imagery.Development of robust matching and transformation modules.Documentation and research paper technical report on findings. Reusable software module API for integration into existing systems.

name: Regional Remote Sensing Center-North, NRSC, ISRO - Electronics
project_details:
Autonomous Navigation systems for UAV plantforms A Simulation based study
The project will work on autonomous navigation for UAVs using simulation tools. The intern will implement algorithms for perception, path planning and control to help drones navigate complex environments. This includeds building vision or sensor based systems to understand the surroundings and developing navigation logic. The exact direction like obstcle avoidance optimal path finding working with mulitple drones , indoor scenarios, or using machine learning can be decided after some initial reading and discussions based on whats technically feasible and interesting. Required skills and Traits Good coding skills in python basic knowledge of computer vision or robotics concepts solid grasp of algorithms and data structures problem solving mindset ability to work independently decent writing and documentation habits.Expected Outcomes: working simulation setup for testing UAV navigation Performance analysis comparing different methods clean documented code with proper README. Technical reports covering approach results and what could be good enough for publication.

name: Regional Remote Sensing Center-North, NRSC, ISRO - IT
project_details:
Computer Vision and machine learning 3D Point Cloud data processing (acquired from Terrestrial Laser Scanning) for estimation of Tree Structural Parameters,Machine learning based dominant tree species mapping using Earth Observation Datasets,Crop Biophysical Parameter Retrieval using Remote Sensing data,Crop yield estimation using Machine learning approaches ,Sugarcane Crop Cultivars Discrimination Using Satellite Data and Machine Learning,Optimizing Emergency Response Through Spatially Accurate Addressing, GIS Analytics, and Predictive Modelling.
3D Point cloud data is acquired from Terrestrial Laser Scanner of different target objects and surroundings. Various Computer Vision and machine learning algorithms can be applied to this dataset to estimate various parameters of tree structure.Required Skills & Traits: Creativity and Curiosity (for algorithm development)Expected Outcomes: python package for estimation of tree parameters using point cloud data.Specific courses required for project execution: Computer Vision, Machine Learning, Python Programming,Description: Different Tree species appear differently in satellite imagery. Machine learning algorithms can help us understand the habitat/ecological niche of various tree species and help us map the spread of various species at various scales.Required Skills & Traits: Creativity and Curiosity (for algorithm development)Expected Outcomes: Scripts, maps and publication on this topic.Specific courses required for project execution:  Machine Learning, Python Programming, Digital Image Processing,Description: In this focuses will be on estimating key crop biophysical parameters using satellite- or drone-based remote sensing data. Students will work with multispectral and/or hyperspectral imagery to derive parameters such as LAI, biomass, chlorophyll content, and other relevant vegetation indicators.Required Skills & Traits: Language - Python and Jupyter Notebook, Libraries - Gdal, Geopandas, etc.Expected Outcomes: Biophysical parameter estimation  Specific Courses Required for Project Execution: Programming in Python, ML & DL algorithms, Remote Sensing Fundamentals, Image processing,In this crop yield models will be generated using satellite, or drone based remote sensing data along with the agrometeorological  and crop management data. Required Skills & Traits: Language - Python and Jupyter Notebook, Libraries - Gdal, Geopandas, etc.   DL Framewok  - Tensor Flow / KerasExpected Outcomes: Crop yield estimation Specific Courses Required for Project Execution: Programming in Python, ML & DL algorithms, Remote Sensing Fundamentals, Image processing,Description: The project aims to distinguish sugarcane cultivars using multi-temporal satellite imagery and ML algorithms. Work includes preprocessing satellite data, extracting spectral/vegetation features, and training classification models. The focus area can be refined after initial review of available datasets.Required Skills & Traits: Basic Python programming and familiarity with remote sensing tools. Understanding of machine learning concepts and ability to handle geospatial data. Good analytical and documentation skills.Expected Outcomes: A processed satellite dataset and feature extraction workflow. ML models demonstrating discrimination between sugarcane cultivars with accuracy metrics. A short technical report and well-documented code repository.Specific Courses Required: Python Programming and Machine Learning.,To enhance the functioning of emergency services like ambulance, using precise digital address information in conjunction with geospatial and analytical tools. Geocoded address data, satellite data, road networks, and available historical emergency events can be utilised to help emergency response teams reach the location quickly. It will do so by providing accurate response-time predictions and suggesting the most efficient routes for first responders. It will demonstrate how spatial analysis, remote sensing, and machine-learning models can help overcome the delays that often arise from vague or incomplete addresses.Required Skills & Traits: Web development / Full-stack development, GIS and geospatial analytics, Remote sensing data interpretation (Desirable), Machine Learning & Data Science, Mobile application development, Database management (PostgreSQL/PostGIS)Expected Outcomes: Geospatial Decision Support System (DSS) with spatial analytics. Mobile app for first responders (navigation, logging, geo-verification). Predictive response time models using ML (R&D Component). Optimised routing maps. Spatial hotspot (Repeated Road accidents, fire incidents) and underserved zone (Long travel time due to road/traffic, areas far from emergency services) identification using GIS (R&D Component). Comparative analysis showing improvements using spatially accurate addressingSpecific Courses Required for Project Execution: Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning, Web Development, Database Management Systems, Data Science & Analytics, Fundamentals of GIS / Remote Sensing (Desirable)

name: Regional Remote Sensing Center-North, NRSC, ISRO - IT
project_details:
Downscaling of CMIP6 Temperature and Precipitation Simulations over India using Deep Learning Methods
Description: GCMs simulate global climate processes but their coarse resolution limits regional applicability, especially for diverse terrains like India. To overcome this, a deep-learning-based downscaling of CMIP6 precipitation and temperature using IMD observations is proposed, providing a high-resolution alternative to NEXGDDP for impact assessment studiesRequired Skills & Traits: Python Programming (Numpy, Scipy, Tensorflow, PyTorch, GDAL, NetCDF4)Expected Outcomes: Downscaled Precipitation and Air Temperature Product at 0.25 degree grid size for IndiaSpecific Courses Required for Project Execution: Introduction to Pattern Recognition/ Machine Learning, Machine Learning II/ Deep Learning
